audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Lazarus, Martha/Mary, resurrection and life I am, Jesus weeping, Lazarus call, council plot, Caiaphas saying, Ephraim withdrawal.

verses.restored: Now a certain one was weak, Lazarus from Bethany, from the village of Mary and Martha her sister.
It was Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was weak.
The sisters therefore sent to him, saying, Lord, look, the one you love is weak.
Hearing this, Jesus said, This weakness is not toward death, but for the glory of the Divine, so that the Son of the Divine may be glorified through it.
Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister and Lazarus.
When therefore he heard that he was weak, he remained two days in the place where he was.
Then after this he says to the disciples, Let us go into Judea again.
The disciples say to him, Rabbi, now the Judeans were seeking to stone you, and you are going there again?
Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If anyone walks in the day, one does not stumble, because one sees the light of this world.
But if anyone walks in the night, one stumbles, because the light is not in that one.
He said these things, and after this he says to them, Lazarus our friend has fallen asleep, but I go so that I may awaken him.
The disciples therefore said to him, Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will be saved/healed.
Jesus had spoken about his death, but they thought he was speaking about the rest of sleep.
Then Jesus therefore said to them openly, Lazarus died.
And I rejoice for you, so that you may trust, that I was not there. But let us go to him.
Thomas, called Twin, said to the fellow disciples, Let us also go, so that we may die with him.
Coming therefore, Jesus found him already four days having been in the tomb.
Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia away.
Many of the Judeans had come to Martha and Mary to console them concerning their brother.
Martha therefore, when she heard that Jesus was coming, met him; but Mary was sitting in the house.
Martha said to Jesus, Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
But even now I know that whatever you ask the Divine, the Divine will give you.
Jesus says to her, Your brother will rise.
Martha says to him, I know that he will rise in the resurrection on the last day.
Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life; the one trusting into me, even if that one dies, will live.
And everyone living and trusting into me will certainly not die into the age. Do you trust this?
She says to him, Yes, Lord; I have trusted that you are the Messiah, the Son of the Divine, the one coming into the world.
Saying this, she went away and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Teacher is here and calls you.
When that one heard, she rose quickly and came to him.
Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was still in the place where Martha met him.
The Judeans who were with her in the house consoling her, seeing Mary rise quickly and go out, followed her, supposing that she was going to the tomb to weep there.
Mary therefore, when she came where Jesus was and saw him, fell at his feet, saying to him, Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
Jesus therefore, when he saw her weeping and the Judeans who came with her weeping, was deeply moved in spirit and troubled himself.
He said, Where have you laid him? They say to him, Lord, come and see.
Jesus wept.
The Judeans therefore were saying, See how he loved him.
But some of them said, Could not this one, who opened the eyes of the blind, also have made it so this one would not die?
Jesus therefore, again deeply moved in himself, comes to the tomb. It was a cave, and a stone lay against it.
Jesus says, Take away the stone. Martha, the sister of the one who had died, says to him, Lord, by now there will be odor, for it is the fourth day.
Jesus says to her, Did I not say to you that if you trust, you will see the glory of the Divine?
They took away the stone. Jesus lifted up his eyes and said, Father, I thank you that you heard me.
I knew that you always hear me, but because of the crowd standing around I said it, so that they may trust that you sent me.
Saying these things, he cried out with a great voice, Lazarus, come out.
The one who had died came out, bound feet and hands with burial cloths, and his face wrapped in a cloth. Jesus says to them, Loose him and let him go.
Therefore many of the Judeans who had come to Mary and beheld what he did trusted into him.
But some of them went away to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
Therefore the chief priests and Pharisees gathered a council and were saying, What are we doing? For this human does many signs.
If we leave him thus, all will trust into him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and nation.
But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, You know nothing,
nor do you reason that it is advantageous for you that one human should die for the people, and not the whole nation perish.
He did not say this from himself, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus was about to die for the nation,
and not for the nation only, but also so that the scattered children of the Divine might be gathered into one.
From that day therefore they took counsel to kill him.
Jesus therefore no longer walked openly among the Judeans, but went away from there into the region near the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there he remained with the disciples.
The Passover of the Judeans was near, and many went up to Jerusalem from the country before the Passover to purify themselves.
They were seeking Jesus and saying with one another as they stood in the temple, What do you think? That he will certainly not come to the feast?
Now the chief priests and Pharisees had given commands that if anyone knew where he was, that one should report it, so that they might seize him.
